answer:
The Datadog MCP server is a bridge that connects AI agents to your Datadog observability data, enabling agents to query metrics, logs, monitors, and dashboards directly [1][2]. By using the Model Context Protocol (MCP), it allows AI applications like Claude Desktop, Cursor, and Windsurf to retrieve insights or perform actions through standardized tools [1][2].

Datadog offers an official MCP server, which is recommended for managed onboarding and Datadog SSO [3]. Additionally, several community-developed versions exist that offer expanded features like full CRUD (create, read, update, delete) operations, self-hosting options, or specific performance optimizations [3][4][5][6][7].

When using an MCP server with an AI agent, you typically configure it by providing your Datadog API and application keys [3]. To maintain efficiency, most implementations use techniques like tool allowlists or pagination based on token budgets to ensure observability data does not overwhelm the agent's context window [1][8][3].

results.title: What Is an MCP Server (Model Context Protocol Server)? | Datadog
Designing MCP tools for agents: Lessons from building Datadog’s MCP server | Datadog
